name: Egyptian pumpkin pie
description: This Egyptian pumpkin pie elevates the classic by adding a luxurious layer of sweet pumpkin flavoured bechamel that takes it to the next level. One bite of this creamy layered masterpiece and you'll be craving it all fall long.

ingredients.ingredient: pumpkin (sweet variety) peeled and sliced into 1 inch cubes
granulated sugar (1/4 of the weight of the pumpkin cubes)

steps: Toast some walnuts and hazelnuts in the oven for 5 to 10 minutes, and take them out when they're golden.
Blitz digestive biscuits, toasted walnuts, flour, sugar, salt, desiccated coconut, and cinnamon in a food processor until it reaches a sandy texture.
Stream in the butter while the machine is running until it forms clumps.
Press the biscuit base into a 10-in spring form pan, using a ramekin to press the biscuit base against the side of the pan to form a wall.
Bake the base in the oven for about 15 minutes.
Cook the pumpkin cubes (which have been macerating in sugar overnight) in their liquid over medium heat covered until soft (about 15 to 20 minutes).
Strain the pumpkin, saving the liquid, and set the pumpkin aside.
Measure out 500ml of pumpkin liquid, topping up with water if necessary.
In the same pan, melt the butter for the bechamel on medium heat, add flour, and cook for a few minutes.
Reduce to low heat and slowly add milk while constantly whisking.
Slowly add the pumpkin syrup, whisking until smooth.
Increase the heat, bring to a simmer until it thickens, then add the cream and vanilla bean paste.
Fill the cooled base with the pumpkin (about 2/3 of the way up) and pour the bechamel on top.
Bake at 180°C for about half an hour or until the top browns.
Prepare the topping by chopping toasted hazelnuts and mixing them with sultanas, light brown sugar, desiccated coconut, cinnamon, a pinch of salt, and melted butter.
Let the pie cool for about an hour or an hour and a half until it sets before serving.
